title: change `language_detection_threshold` default value
body: the current value of `language_detection_threshold` means that `language_detection_segments` will be 1 regardless of the user set value, the new value will ensure that `language_detection_segments` is respected

step_plan.summary: Change the default for WhisperModel.transcribe's language_detection_threshold from None to 1.0 so the language-detection loop no longer breaks immediately and the language_detection_segments parameter is honored by default.

steps.operation: Change default value of language_detection_threshold and update docstring
steps.reason: The current default of language_detection_threshold=None makes the check (language_detection_threshold is None or language_probability > language_detection_threshold) short-circuit true on the first segment, causing the loop to break immediately and ignoring language_detection_segments. Setting the default to a numeric value (1.0) prevents the 'is None' short-circuit and ensures the loop will consider up to language_detection_segments segments by default.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral change in default language-detection: callers that relied on the previous implicit early-exit behavior will now have language detection run across multiple segments unless they explicitly pass a threshold. Unit tests that assumed the previous behavior may need updates. Public API signature remains backwards-compatible in type (Optional[float]) but the runtime default behavior changes; downstream code relying on the old default may observe different detected languages. No other code paths or modules should be affected beyond language-detection behavior, but integration tests and any consumer libraries should be checked.
